articles.headline: Banks face loss of fee income due to RBI circular
articles.summary: Banks face a loss of fee income from interbank transactions due to an RBI circular on interoperable free deposits, impacting profitability. Large lenders would lose Rs 3,000-4,000 crore, while smaller ones would lose Rs 500-1,000 crore.

articles.headline: Electronics exports to hit new high in June quarter
articles.summary: Electronics exports are set to hit a new high in the June quarter, driven by companies like Apple, Samsung, and Dixon. India's electronics exports reached $2.31 billion in April, up 26.6% year-on-year, showing a strong growth trend.

articles.headline: O&M Cos go slow on 55% ethanol blending rollout
articles.summary: Oil marketing companies are reportedly slowing down the rollout of 55% ethanol blending, impacting India's green energy goals. The government had aimed for an aggressive increase in ethanol-blended petrol.

articles.headline: MERC India seeks more fuel from hydros, blends
articles.summary: The MERC India conference focused on increasing fuel supply from hydroelectric projects and promoting blending options to enhance energy security and reduce dependence on fossil fuels. Experts discussed grid stability and renewable integration challenges.

articles.headline: Many companies join pre-IPO placement queue
articles.summary: Private companies are increasingly opting for pre-IPO placements to raise funds before going public, driven by market conditions and investor interest in late-stage growth. These rounds offer valuation boosts and strategic partnerships.

articles.headline: Google Cloud to host latest AI models in India
articles.summary: Google Cloud will host its latest AI models, including Gemini, in India, partnering with local companies and startups. This move aims to leverage India's talent pool and growing AI market, enhancing digital infrastructure and AI capabilities across the region.

articles.headline: India, Indonesia boost defence ties with BrahMos, maritime pacts
articles.summary: India and Indonesia enhanced defense ties, signing agreements on BrahMos missile exports and maritime security cooperation. The collaboration aims to strengthen regional stability and counter illegal activities in the Indo-Pacific.

articles.headline: India explores options for energy collaboration
articles.summary: India is exploring options for energy collaboration, including renewable energy projects and enhancing grid connectivity. Discussions focus on green hydrogen and sustainable energy solutions to meet growing demands and reduce carbon emissions.

articles.headline: Dispute resolution mechanism to be established
articles.summary: The rapprochement between India and Indonesia will establish an effective dispute resolution mechanism. This aims to facilitate smoother trade and investment, ensuring timely resolution of commercial disputes and improving the business environment between the two nations.

articles.headline: Overseas stars in Swiggy below 10% for first time
articles.summary: The share of overseas employees in Swiggy's star ratings fell below 10% for the first time. This reflects a growing reliance on domestic talent and localization strategies. The company is focusing on strengthening its India-based workforce.

articles.headline: 5 dead in Wayanad landslide, cause debated
articles.summary: A landslide in Kalikad, Wayanad, killed five, including four tunnel site workers. Experts debate whether the landslide was natural or man-made due to quarrying and construction. Rescue operations are ongoing.

articles.headline: MEA withholds response after ET report on Chinese rail
articles.summary: The Ministry of External Affairs withheld a response to allegations regarding Chinese rail projects, deeming them "baseless and shocking" after the Economic Times submitted its final report. This follows an ongoing investigation into allegations of corruption.

articles.headline: Congress seeks Ram Temple Trust's dissolution
articles.summary: The Congress party is demanding the dissolution of the Ram Temple Trust due to alleged irregularities in donations. They seek a comprehensive audit of all funds, escalating the political debate surrounding the temple's construction and finances.

articles.headline: Gehlot demands probe on 'Sonia's disclosure'
articles.summary: Rajasthan CM Ashok Gehlot demanded a probe into the alleged disclosure of Sonia Gandhi's assets, linking it to potential political vendetta. He called for a thorough investigation to ensure transparency and accountability.

articles.headline: All eyes on another Thackeray meeting
articles.summary: Another meeting involving the Thackeray family is anticipated, sparking speculation about political realignments within Maharashtra. Key figures from Shiv Sena factions are expected to discuss strategies amid ongoing political tussles.

articles.headline: Maha Congress leader's remarks create buzz over NCP-SP's plan
articles.summary: Remarks by a Maharashtra Congress leader have fueled speculation about potential strategies between the NCP-Sharadchandra Pawar (SP) faction and Congress. This indicates ongoing efforts to consolidate opposition forces ahead of upcoming elections.

articles.headline: Evidence suggests decision for Woman Congress VP was planned
articles.summary: Evidence suggests that the decision to appoint a woman as Congress Vice President was a pre-planned move to address gender imbalance and rejuvenate the party's image. This strategic decision aims to attract more women voters and leaders.

articles.headline: Heavy rainfall triggers flash floods in J&K
articles.summary: Heavy rainfall has triggered flash floods and landslides in Jammu & Kashmir, particularly in Srinagar. Several areas are waterlogged, causing significant disruption to daily life. Authorities have issued warnings and are conducting rescue operations.

articles.headline: Doda-Kishtwar highway blocked by landslides
articles.summary: The Doda-Kishtwar National Highway is blocked due to multiple landslides triggered by heavy rainfall. Commuters are stranded, and restoration efforts are underway. This disruption impacts connectivity in the region.

articles.headline: India, Indonesia see sea as bridge for future ties
articles.summary: PM Modi emphasized India and Indonesia view the sea as a bridge, not a separator, signifying deeper bilateral ties. This highlights their shared vision for maritime cooperation and regional connectivity, fostering economic and strategic partnerships.

articles.headline: PM Jokowi asserts 100% Indonesian identity on India visit
articles.summary: Indonesian President Joko Widodo jokingly asserted his 100% Indonesian identity during his India visit, emphasizing strong cultural and historical ties between the two nations. This underscores a warm bilateral relationship and shared heritage.

articles.headline: School dropout rate falls; teacher strength crosses 1 crore
articles.summary: India's school dropout rate has significantly declined, and the number of teachers has surpassed 1 crore. This improvement is attributed to government initiatives like Beti Bachao Beti Padhao, infrastructure upgrades, and increased enrollment.

articles.headline: India focuses on development, not expansion: PM in Jakarta
articles.summary: PM Modi, speaking in Jakarta, reiterated India's foreign policy prioritizes development and peace over expansionism. He emphasized collaboration, economic growth, and regional stability, highlighting India's commitment to a rules-based international order.

articles.headline: IIM-B opens first offshore campus in Indonesia
articles.summary: IIM Bangalore is establishing its first offshore campus in Indonesia, marking a significant step in international education. The campus will offer management programs, fostering educational exchange and strengthening bilateral ties in education.

articles.headline: Andaman & Nicobar, crucible for Indian identity
articles.summary: The Andaman & Nicobar Islands have served as a crucible for Indian national identity, reflecting diverse cultures and historical significance. PM Modi highlighted their role in India's freedom struggle and strategic importance.

articles.headline: India, Indonesia form IPEF governing council
articles.summary: India and Indonesia have established a governing council for the Indo-Pacific Economic Forum (IPEF), aiming to keep markets open for trade and strengthen economic cooperation in the region. This initiative promotes a free and open Indo-Pacific.

articles.headline: India promotes gender equality, women's empowerment
articles.summary: The Indian government has launched several initiatives to promote gender equality and women's empowerment, including educational programs, economic schemes, and legal reforms. These efforts aim to bridge the gender gap and enhance women's participation in all sectors.

articles.headline: India ASTRA deal may open big export market
articles.summary: The Astra missile deal for Air to Air Missile Cooperative Arrangement (AAMCA) could open a significant export market for India's defense sector. This collaboration highlights India's growing capabilities in missile technology and defense manufacturing.

articles.headline: Khandre rejects move to acquire HMT forest land
articles.summary: Karnataka Minister Eshwar Khandre rejected a proposal to acquire HMT forest land for a residential layout, emphasizing environmental protection. The decision highlights the government's commitment to preserving natural resources and safeguarding biodiversity.

articles.headline: SC to DMK: Should we regulate CM?
articles.summary: The Supreme Court questioned the DMK government on whether it should regulate the Chief Minister, specifically in a case involving an alleged corruption scandal. This query probes the extent of judicial intervention in executive functions.

articles.headline: Faith emerges amid receding waters in Kedarnath
articles.summary: As floodwaters recede in Kedarnath, faith and recovery efforts emerge. Pilgrims are returning, and rebuilding work is underway. This signifies resilience in the face of natural disaster and the continued importance of the pilgrimage site.

articles.headline: Keeping watch on drought-like situation, says DK
articles.summary: DK Shivakumar stated that Karnataka is monitoring a drought-like situation, with contingency plans being prepared. The government is assessing rainfall deficits and their impact on agriculture to mitigate potential crises.

articles.headline: ECI to examine complaints into SIR manipulation
articles.summary: The Election Commission of India (ECI) has pledged to examine complaints regarding alleged manipulation of Special Investigation Reports (SIRs). This comes amid concerns over election integrity and transparency.

articles.headline: Cognizant gets relief in trademark, copyright dispute
articles.summary: Cognizant received relief in a trademark and copyright dispute with Atyati Technologies. The court ruling favored Cognizant, resolving a contentious intellectual property battle concerning software and brand identity.

articles.headline: CCI clears upGrad-Unacademy merger in edtech
articles.summary: The Competition Commission of India (CCI) approved the merger between upGrad and Unacademy, consolidating two major players in the edtech sector. This clearance is expected to reshape the online education landscape, fostering competition and innovation.

articles.headline: Rights group seeks NHRC intervention in maternal deaths
articles.summary: A rights group has sought intervention from the National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) regarding increasing maternal and foetal deaths. The plea highlights concerns over healthcare access and quality, urging the NHRC to investigate and ensure accountability.

articles.headline: FDI inflows to India up 44% to $39B in 2025: UN
articles.summary: FDI inflows to India are projected to increase by 44% to $39 billion in 2025, according to a UN report. This surge is attributed to India's strong economic growth, investor-friendly policies, and robust domestic demand.

articles.headline: KMF issues short term tender notification
articles.summary: The Karnataka Milk Federation (KMF) has issued a short term tender notification for various products. This is part of their procurement process, inviting bids from eligible suppliers.

articles.headline: FDI inflows to India, Indonesia hit 20-year low
articles.summary: Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) inflows to India and Indonesia have declined to a 20-year low, impacting economic growth. This dip is attributed to global economic uncertainties and geopolitical factors.

articles.headline: Downstream risks to FDI inflows identified
articles.summary: The UN report identifies "downstream risks" that could impact FDI inflows, including geopolitical tensions, supply chain disruptions, and global economic slowdowns. These factors may deter investors despite positive long-term outlook.

articles.headline: Cumulative rainfall data from June 1 to July 4
articles.summary: Data shows cumulative rainfall from June 1 to July 4 across 36 states and UTs. This provides a snapshot of monsoon progress, essential for agriculture and water management, indicating regional variations in precipitation levels.

articles.headline: State-wise cumulative rainfall performance data
articles.summary: The article presents state-wise cumulative rainfall data, showing percentage departures from normal. This highlights regions with surplus or deficit rainfall, crucial for assessing agricultural prospects and water resource management.

articles.headline: Rainfall activity to intensify over next 3 days
articles.summary: Rainfall activity is expected to intensify across central and western India over the next three days, according to weather forecasts. This intensification is crucial for monsoon-dependent agriculture.

articles.headline: RBI mulls guidelines on property title searches
articles.summary: The RBI is considering new guidelines for property title searches to enhance transparency and reduce risks in real estate transactions. This aims to standardize the process, improve due diligence, and protect lenders and buyers.

articles.headline: Standardizing property title checks: Benefits
articles.summary: Standardizing property title checks would benefit banks by reducing fraud, increasing lending confidence, and improving asset quality. It would streamline property transactions and enhance legal certainty for buyers and financial institutions.

articles.headline: Ministries asked to fill CPSE vacancies in 3 months
articles.summary: Government ministries have been directed to fill all top vacancies in Central Public Sector Enterprises (CPSEs) within three months. This initiative aims to improve governance, operational efficiency, and accelerate decision-making in public sector undertakings.

articles.headline: India asks US to reconsider 12.5% additional tariff
articles.summary: India has urged the US to reconsider its proposed 12.5% additional tariff on certain goods. This request aims to prevent negative impacts on bilateral trade and maintain fair economic relations between the two countries.

articles.headline: FM urges toymakers to target 25% global market share
articles.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman urged Indian toymakers to aim for a 25% share of the global market. She emphasized the importance of innovation, quality, and 'Make in India' initiatives to boost exports and domestic manufacturing.

articles.headline: EAC-PM calls for rapid scale-up of battery storage
articles.summary: An EAC-PM paper advocates for a rapid scale-up of battery storage solutions to support India's renewable energy transition and grid stability. It recommends policy incentives and investment in manufacturing and R&D for energy storage.

articles.headline: Info & Computer Services to make up 1/5th of planned index
articles.summary: The Information & Computer Services sector is set to comprise one-fifth of a newly planned index, reflecting its growing economic importance. This inclusion highlights the sector's significant contribution to GDP and market capitalization.

articles.headline: India's debt market crucial for economic stability
articles.summary: The article discusses the critical role of India's debt market in ensuring economic stability and attracting foreign investment. It highlights the need for reforms to deepen the market, enhance liquidity, and manage debt sustainably for long-term growth.

articles.headline: AI economy: India is a prime candidate
articles.summary: The article posits that AI's impact extends beyond technology to reshape the economy. India is a prime candidate to lead this AI-driven economic transformation, with its vast talent pool and digital infrastructure, fostering innovation and new business models.

articles.headline: The ugly and beautiful sides of life
articles.summary: This opinion piece explores the dualities of life, focusing on its unpleasant and beautiful aspects. It reflects on how challenges and joys shape human experience, urging readers to appreciate both the struggles and the moments of grace.

articles.headline: Kerala's development model as a silver lining for India
articles.summary: Kerala's unique development model, characterized by high social indicators and decentralized governance, offers a "silver lining playbook" for India. The article explores how Kerala's approach to health, education, and human development provides valuable lessons for national progress.

articles.headline: Minimising 'tokenmaxxing' for effective AI
articles.summary: The article argues against "tokenmaxxing" – optimizing AI solely for minimal token use – advocating for a broader, human-centric approach to AI development. It emphasizes practical utility and ethical considerations over mere efficiency metrics for real-world impact.

articles.headline: Belgium urges caution on US, UK political moves
articles.summary: This opinion piece advises Belgium to exercise caution regarding political decisions made by the US and UK. It highlights the need for independent foreign policy and strategic thinking amidst global political shifts.

articles.headline: Diversification strategy: Put both eggs in one basket
articles.summary: This article challenges conventional wisdom on diversification, suggesting that sometimes concentrating resources in a single, high-conviction area can yield superior returns. It explores the nuances of risk management and strategic focus in investing.

articles.headline: No compliant ownership is leadership
articles.summary: This article argues that true leadership requires taking ownership beyond mere compliance. It emphasizes proactive responsibility, ethical governance, and a commitment to stakeholders for sustainable success in business.

articles.headline: Mumbai Airport duty-free pouches in breach of law
articles.summary: Duty-free pouches at Mumbai Airport are allegedly in breach of law for mislabeling and non-compliance with regulations. This issue raises concerns about consumer rights and regulatory oversight in the retail sector.

articles.headline: OMCS wary of E85 expansion near zero
articles.summary: Oil Marketing Companies (OMCs) are hesitant about the E85 ethanol blend expansion, citing infrastructure challenges and demand uncertainties. The goal to increase ethanol blending faces practical hurdles.

articles.headline: Global giants resist disclosures of private pacts
articles.summary: Global technology giants are resisting mandatory disclosures of private commercial agreements, citing confidentiality and competitive reasons. This stance clashes with government efforts to increase transparency and curb anti-competitive practices.

articles.headline: ₹13k-cr investment for upgrading five steel plants
articles.summary: Five major steel plants are set to receive ₹13,000 crore investment for modernization and capacity upgradation. This aims to boost domestic steel production, enhance efficiency, and reduce import dependence.

articles.summary: Gold prices tumbled by ₹1,400, reaching ₹1.49 lakh per 10 grams, influenced by global market trends and investor sentiment. This significant drop impacts jewelers and investors.

articles.headline: Jain's ex-aide joins executive panel
articles.summary: A former aide to Satendra Jain has been appointed to an executive panel, drawing attention amid political discussions. This appointment may signal shifts in political alliances or strategy.

articles.headline: Swiggy cuts foreign stake below 50% for margins
articles.summary: Swiggy has reduced its foreign ownership stake to below 50% to unlock higher margins from its quick commerce business. This strategic move aims to optimize financial performance and comply with regulatory requirements for foreign investment.

articles.summary: The RBI is introducing interoperability in the Trade Receivables Discounting System (TReDS) to prevent monopolies and increase participation. This will allow MSMEs to access multiple financiers and ensure fair competition in bill discounting.

articles.headline: Flipkart cuts seller fees, no strings attached
articles.summary: Flipkart has announced cuts in seller fees without additional conditions, aiming to attract more vendors and boost sales. This move intensifies competition in the e-commerce sector and supports small businesses.

articles.summary: RBI Adage raised ₹230 crore from Indred and other investors, signaling strong market confidence. The funding aims to support its expansion plans and technological advancements in the financial sector.

articles.headline: E-commerce eyes festive revival after mixed sales
articles.summary: E-commerce platforms are anticipating a festive revival after experiencing mixed sales performance. Companies are implementing aggressive strategies, including discounts and new product launches, to boost consumer spending during the upcoming festive season.

articles.summary: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq declined amid persistent fears about the AI chip sector. Investors are concerned about high valuations and potential oversupply, leading to a cautious market sentiment in technology stocks.

articles.summary: Cochin Shipyard's Q3 share sale received six times the bids, demonstrating strong investor confidence. The government exercised the greenshoe option, indicating robust demand for the shares.

articles.summary: An RBI order related to SMS charges could dent banks' income by ₹300 crore, impacting their profitability. The directive aims to streamline communication costs for customers but poses a revenue challenge for financial institutions.

articles.summary: Irdai has directed state-run insurers to properly account for wage costs, ensuring transparency and accuracy in financial reporting. This move aims to improve governance and bring their accounting practices in line with regulatory standards.

articles.summary: The Ministry of Environment launched a new initiative to conserve and protect wetlands across the country.

articles.headline: Parliament discusses economic reforms
articles.summary: Parliamentary sessions focused on discussing recent economic reforms and their impact on various sectors.

articles.headline: Scientists achieve breakthrough in fusion energy
articles.summary: Scientists have achieved a significant breakthrough in fusion energy research, moving closer to sustainable power.

articles.headline: New policy to boost domestic tourism
articles.summary: The government announced a new policy with incentives to promote domestic tourism and boost the hospitality sector.

articles.headline: Parliamentary committee reviews social justice programs
articles.summary: A parliamentary panel reviewed the effectiveness of social justice programs and welfare schemes.

articles.headline: India-Australia ties deepen with new pact
articles.summary: Bilateral ties between India and Australia have deepened with the signing of a new economic cooperation agreement.

articles.headline: Cricket team announces new coach
articles.summary: The Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) announced the appointment of a new head coach for the national team.

articles.headline: New policy to promote rural development
articles.summary: The government announced a new policy focused on accelerating rural development and improving livelihoods.

articles.headline: India-Canada strategic partnership reviewed
articles.summary: High-level talks between India and Canada reviewed their strategic partnership and future cooperation.

articles.headline: Ahmedabad Blasts: HC upholds death for 38, lifer for 11
articles.summary: The Gujarat High Court upheld the death penalty for 38 convicts and life imprisonment for 11 others in the 2008 Ahmedabad serial blasts case. The court also ordered compensation for victims: Rs 25 lakh for deceased, Rs 10 lakh for seriously injured, and Rs 5 lakh for others.

articles.headline: Fourth aide of Bhupender Yadav found dead in UP
articles.summary: A fourth aide of Union Environment Minister Bhupender Yadav was found dead in Uttar Pradesh, continuing a series of mysterious deaths. His body was found near a railway track in Mainpuri. Police are investigating, with previous deaths also under scrutiny.

articles.headline: PNG connections target unviable areas, say gas cos
articles.summary: City gas companies have informed the government that the target to provide 100,000 PNG connections a day is unviable. They cite insufficient incentives and tough regulations. The government aims to connect households to piped natural gas.

articles.headline: Air-cooled condenser firms seek review of Chinese bids
articles.summary: Air-cooled condenser manufacturers are seeking a review of the government's approval for Chinese bids in power projects. They cite national security concerns and unfair competition, advocating for protection of domestic industry and jobs.

articles.headline: US remains India's top auto parts export market
articles.summary: The US remains India's primary export market for auto parts, despite ongoing tariff challenges. India's auto parts exports to the US grew, underscoring the sector's resilience and strong bilateral trade ties.

articles.headline: India, Indonesia ink MoUs on critical minerals, Sabang port
articles.summary: India and Indonesia signed MoUs on critical minerals and the industrial development of Sabang port. These agreements aim to boost bilateral trade, secure supply chains, and enhance strategic connectivity in the Indo-Pacific region.

articles.headline: Cost pressure to keep Nifty 50 Q1 profit muted
articles.summary: Nifty 50 companies are expected to report muted Q1 profit growth due to persistent cost pressures, including rising input costs and wage inflation. Despite revenue growth, profitability remains constrained, affecting investor sentiment.

articles.headline: NBFC credit growth 14% in May, led by retail, farm loans
articles.summary: NBFC credit grew by 14% in May, primarily driven by strong demand for retail and farm loans. This growth indicates a robust financial sector and increased credit penetration in key economic segments.
